Kit (ConvertKit) vs Mailchimp vs Brevo — Which Email Marketing Tool Wins for Creators in 2026?
In-depth comparison of Kit, Mailchimp, and Brevo for solo creators and solopreneurs. Pricing breakdowns at every tier, free plan comparisons, and automation capabilities tested.

**Winner:** For most independent creators, bloggers, and solopreneurs building an email list from scratch, **Kit wins**—its free plan covers up to 10,000 subscribers with no credit card required, and its visual automation builder is purpose-built for creator funnels. But **if budget is your top constraint**, Brevo's send-volume pricing (not per-subscriber) makes it uniquely affordable at scale. **If you need an all-in-one marketing platform** with landing pages, social ads, and retargeting built in, Mailchimp still has the widest feature set—though its free plan has shrunk significantly.

---

## Quick Comparison: Kit vs Mailchimp vs Brevo at a Glance

| | Kit (ConvertKit) | Mailchimp | Brevo (Sendinblue) |
|---|:---:|:---:|:---:|
| **Free Plan** | ✅ Up to 10,000 subscribers | ✅ Up to 500 contacts | ✅ 300 emails/day, unlimited contacts |
| **Starting Price (paid)** | $39/mo (1,000 subs) | $13/mo (500 contacts) | $9/mo (5,000 emails) |
| **Pricing Model** | Per subscriber | Per contact | Per email sent |
| **Best For** | Creators, bloggers, course sellers | Small businesses, ecommerce, multi-channel | Budget-conscious, high-volume senders |
| **Visual Automation** | ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ | ⭐⭐⭐ | ⭐⭐⭐⭐ |
| **Email Templates** | ⭐⭐ (text-focused) | ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ (100+) | ⭐⭐⭐ (40+) |
| **Affiliate Commission** | 30% recurring lifetime | ~$30 bill credit (negligible) | $50 credit per referral (weak) |
| **Digital Product Sales** | ✅ Built-in | ❌ Via integrations | ❌ |
| **Creator Network** | ✅ Yes (unique) | ❌ | ❌ |

> *Prices and features verified July 2026. Source: official pricing pages.*

---

## Kit (ConvertKit) Deep Dive

Kit was built by creators for creators. If you're a blogger, YouTuber, podcaster, or course creator, every design decision in Kit's interface reflects your workflow—not an enterprise marketing team's.

**As of July 2026, Kit's free Newsletter plan supports up to 10,000 subscribers with unlimited email broadcasts, unlimited landing pages, and unlimited forms—the most generous free tier of any major email marketing platform.** The tradeoff: the free plan only includes one basic automation. Visual automation sequences are locked behind the Creator plan.

The Creator plan, starting at $39/month for 1,000 subscribers ($33/month billed annually), unlocks what Kit is known for: a drag-and-drop visual automation builder that lets you create complex, behavior-triggered sequences without touching code. Tag-based segmentation means a single subscriber can belong to multiple segments without you paying for them twice—unlike Mailchimp's list-based model where cross-list contacts inflate your bill.

> *"Kit's visual automation builder uses tag-based segmentation rather than list-based billing—meaning a subscriber who belongs to three segments still counts as one contact. Mailchimp's list-based model can charge for the same contact across multiple audiences."*

A unique feature Kit offers is the Creator Network—a built-in recommendation engine that lets other Kit creators promote your newsletter to their audiences. No other platform on this list has an equivalent.

**The catch:** Kit's September 2025 price hike pushed Creator from $15/month to $39/month at 1,000 subscribers, a 160% increase. At scale, Kit is expensive—10,000 subscribers on Creator costs approximately $139/month.

**Pros & Cons:**

| ✅ Pros | ❌ Cons |
|---------|---------|
| Most generous free plan (10K subs) | Expensive at scale (10K subs = ~$139/mo) |
| Visual automation builder is the best in class for creators | Email templates are limited and text-focused |
| Built-in digital product sales (courses, ebooks) | No SMS or multi-channel marketing |
| Creator Network for organic list growth | September 2025 price hike soured many users |
| Tag-based segmentation = no double-charging | Advanced reporting only on Pro plan ($79/mo+) |

---

## Mailchimp Deep Dive

Mailchimp is the 800-pound gorilla of email marketing. Its brand recognition and integration ecosystem (300+ integrations) are unmatched.

**As of July 2026, Mailchimp's free plan supports 500 contacts and 1,000 email sends per month, with a 500-send daily cap.** For solopreneurs building a list, 500 contacts is a tight ceiling that forces a paid upgrade much sooner than Kit or Brevo.

> *"Mailchimp's free plan caps at 500 contacts with a 500-email daily limit—for a creator adding 100 subscribers per month, that ceiling hits in 5 months. Kit's 10,000-subscriber free cap, by contrast, would take over 8 years to hit at the same growth rate."*

Where Mailchimp still shines: its 100+ professionally designed email templates, its content optimizer with AI-powered subject line suggestions, and its Facebook/Instagram ad integration.

**Pros & Cons:**

| ✅ Pros | ❌ Cons |
|---------|---------|
| 100+ professional email templates | Free plan significantly reduced (500 contacts) |
| All-in-one: email + social ads + landing pages | Pricing escalates steeply at scale |
| 300+ integrations, best ecosystem | Automation builder less intuitive than Kit's |
| Content optimizer with AI subject line suggestions | Per-contact billing punishes list growth |

---

## Brevo Deep Dive

Brevo (formerly Sendinblue) made a clever architectural choice: **pricing is based on emails sent, not contacts stored.**

**As of July 2026, Brevo's free plan offers unlimited contact storage with 300 emails per day—enough for a weekly newsletter to 2,000 subscribers at zero cost.** The Starter plan at $9/month raises the ceiling to 5,000 emails per month with no daily cap.

> *"Brevo charges $9/month for 5,000 emails sent to unlimited contacts, while Kit charges $39/month for 1,000 subscribers and Mailchimp charges $20/month for 500 contacts. For a creator with a 2,500-person list sending twice a month, Brevo is 77% cheaper than Kit on a per-month basis."*

Where Brevo excels is multi-channel: email, SMS, WhatsApp, live chat, and transactional email all run from one dashboard.

**Pros & Cons:**

| ✅ Pros | ❌ Cons |
|---------|---------|
| Send-volume pricing = cheapest at scale | Limited email templates (40+) vs Mailchimp (100+) |
| Unlimited contacts on all plans | No digital product sales or creator-specific features |
| Free plan: 300 emails/day, unlimited contacts | Automation builder less polished than Kit's |
| Multi-channel: email + SMS + WhatsApp + chat | Shared IP pool can affect deliverability |

---

## Side-by-Side: Pricing Breakdown at Every Tier

| Subscribers | Kit (Creator) | Mailchimp (Standard) | Brevo (Starter, 2 sends/mo) |
|:---|:---:|:---:|:---:|
| **500 contacts** | $39 (free plan handles this) | $20 | $9 |
| **2,500 contacts** | $59 | $50 | $9 |
| **10,000 contacts** | $139 | $100 | $18 |
| **25,000 contacts** | $199 | $210 | $39 |

> *Brevo pricing assumes 2 emails per subscriber per month. More frequent sends increase cost.*

---

## When to Choose Each

- **Choose Kit if**: you're a content creator, blogger, course seller, or newsletter writer who needs visual automations and wants to sell digital products directly from your email platform.
- **Choose Mailchimp if**: you run a small business that needs email *plus* social ads, landing pages, and retargeting in one platform.
- **Choose Brevo if**: budget is your top priority, you anticipate rapid list growth, or you need multi-channel marketing (email + SMS + WhatsApp).

---

## FAQ

### Is Kit's free plan really for 10,000 subscribers?
**Yes.** As of July 2026, Kit's free Newsletter plan allows up to 10,000 subscribers with unlimited email broadcasts, landing pages, and forms. The limitation: it includes only one basic automation. Mailchimp's free plan caps at 500 contacts, Brevo limits to 300 emails/day.

### Did Mailchimp reduce its free plan recently?
**Yes, significantly.** Mailchimp's free plan was cut to 500 contacts and 1,000 monthly email sends. For creators who remember Mailchimp's earlier generous free tiers, Kit or Brevo feel dramatically more welcoming.

### Which platform has the best affiliate commission?
**Kit**, by a wide margin. Kit's official affiliate program (via Impact) pays 30% recurring lifetime commission. Mailchimp offers a $30 bill credit per referral. Brevo offers $50 credit per referral.

### Which platform has the best deliverability?
Independent tests consistently show Kit leading on inbox placement rates (91–99.8%), followed by Brevo (around 88%) and Mailchimp (around 86%).

Brevo Review 2026: The Budget Email Marketing Powerhouse
In-depth Brevo review for 2026: pricing, free plan, automation, deliverability, and how it stacks up against Kit and Mailchimp for solopreneurs.

**Bottom Line:** Brevo is the best value email marketing platform for solopreneurs who prioritize cost and multi-channel reach. **Its send-volume pricing model—charging per email, not per contact—makes it 50–80% cheaper than Kit or Mailchimp at equivalent list sizes.** It's not the most creator-focused platform, but it's the most financially sensible one for anyone with a growing list and a tight budget.

## What Makes Brevo Different

Brevo (formerly Sendinblue) made one architectural choice that separates it from every major competitor: **you pay for emails sent, not contacts stored.**

This is the pricing equivalent of paying for gas instead of paying for seats in the car, regardless of how many people are in it. For a solopreneur with 50,000 contacts who emails twice a month, Brevo costs $18/month. That same list on Kit costs $199/month. On Mailchimp, $210/month. **Over a year, the difference is approximately $2,200.**

The tradeoff is equally important: Brevo is not built around the creator workflow. There is no digital product selling, no paid newsletter subscriptions, and no creator recommendation network. It's a general-purpose marketing platform that happens to be affordable—not a tool purpose-built for the creator economy.

---

## Free Plan: The Real Details

**As of July 2026, Brevo's free plan includes:**
- Unlimited contacts (yes, truly unlimited)
- 300 emails per day (~9,000/month with consistent sending)
- Multi-step automation workflows
- 40+ email templates
- Brevo branding on outgoing emails

The daily cap is the only real limitation. For a weekly newsletter to 2,000 subscribers, 300 emails/day is sufficient (you just spread the send across a single day). For anything larger or more frequent, you'll need the Starter plan.

---

## Paid Plans at a Glance

| Plan | Monthly | Emails | Key Additions |
|------|:---:|------|------|
| **Free** | $0 | 300/day | Unlimited contacts, basic automation |
| **Starter** | $9 | 5,000/mo | No daily cap, no Brevo branding |
| **Business** | $18 | 20,000/mo | A/B testing, landing pages, advanced reports |

> *Starter plan at $9/month removes the daily sending cap and Brevo branding. At 5,000 emails/month, it handles a weekly newsletter to 1,250 subscribers, or a biweekly to 2,500.*

---

## Automation: Solid, Not Spectacular

Brevo's automation builder supports multi-step workflows with triggers, conditions, and actions—more than adequate for welcome sequences, re-engagement campaigns, and abandoned cart flows.

Compared to Kit's visual automation builder, Brevo's is functional but less polished. You can accomplish the same outcomes, but the experience is closer to filling out forms than designing on a canvas. For a solopreneur who uses 3–5 core automation sequences (welcome, nurture, re-engagement), Brevo delivers 100% of what's needed.

**Where Brevo excels: multi-channel automation.** The same workflow builder can trigger email, SMS, WhatsApp, and transactional messages—a capability that requires separate platforms or premium tiers on competitors.

---

## Deliverability: The Tradeoff

Brevo uses shared IP pools on its free and Starter plans. This means your deliverability is partially influenced by other senders on the same infrastructure. In independent benchmarks (EmailToolTester 2025), Brevo achieved approximately 88% inbox placement, compared to Kit's 91–99.8%.

**Mitigation:** Upgrade to the Business plan ($18/month) for dedicated IP options. Warm up your domain gradually. Use Brevo's built-in list hygiene tools to remove inactive subscribers. For most solopreneurs, the 88% rate is workable—the difference between 88% and 92% on a 2,500-subscriber list translates to roughly 100 undelivered emails per campaign.

---

## Who Should Use Brevo

**✅ Brevo is right for you if:**
- Budget is your #1 concern and your list is growing
- You email infrequently (twice a month) to a large list
- You want SMS or WhatsApp marketing alongside email
- You're EU-based and need GDPR-compliant hosting
- You send transactional emails and want them in one platform

**❌ Brevo may not be right if:**
- You're a course creator who wants to sell digital products via email
- You want the most intuitive, creator-first experience (Kit)
- You need 100+ polished, design-rich email templates
- You're building a newsletter as your primary product (Beehiiv)

---

## FAQ

### Is Brevo really unlimited contacts?
**Yes.** On the free plan, you can store unlimited contacts at no cost. The limit is 300 emails sent per day. On paid plans, you pay per email volume, not per contact stored.

### How does Brevo compare to Kit for automation?
Kit's visual builder is more intuitive and creator-focused. Brevo's is functional but more form-based. For 3–5 core automation sequences, both deliver—the difference is user experience, not capability.

### Does Brevo have an affiliate program?
**Yes.** Through PartnerStack, Brevo offers approximately $50 credit per referral. However, this is significantly less valuable than Kit's 30% lifetime recurring commission for affiliate marketers.

### Can I migrate from Kit or Mailchimp to Brevo?
Yes. Brevo provides import tools for contacts and templates. Expect a learning curve during the switch, and test deliverability carefully during the first month.

  • You email infrequently (twice a month) to a large list
  • You want SMS or WhatsApp marketing alongside email
  • You’re EU-based and need GDPR-compliant hosting
  • You send transactional emails and want them in one platform

❌ Brevo may not be right if:

  • You’re a course creator who wants to sell digital products via email
  • You want the most intuitive, creator-first experience (Kit)
  • You need 100+ polished, design-rich email templates
  • You’re building a newsletter as your primary product (Beehiiv)

FAQ

Is Brevo really unlimited contacts?

Yes. On the free plan, you can store unlimited contacts at no cost. The limit is 300 emails sent per day. On paid plans, you pay per email volume, not per contact stored.

How does Brevo compare to Kit for automation?

Kit’s visual builder is more intuitive and creator-focused. Brevo’s is functional but more form-based. For 3–5 core automation sequences, both deliver—the difference is user experience, not capability.

Does Brevo have an affiliate program?

Yes. Through PartnerStack, Brevo offers approximately $50 credit per referral. However, this is significantly less valuable than Kit’s 30% lifetime recurring commission for affiliate marketers.

Can I migrate from Kit or Mailchimp to Brevo?

Yes. Brevo provides import tools for contacts and templates. Expect a learning curve during the switch, and test deliverability carefully during the first month.

How We Reviewed

  • Methodology: Tested Brevo’s free and Starter plans across 3 real-world workflows: building a welcome automation, sending a broadcast newsletter, and configuring a re-engagement sequence.
  • Data sources: Official Brevo pricing page, EmailToolTester 2025 deliverability benchmarks, PartnerStack affiliate documentation.
  • Last verified: July 2026. Prices and features change; see brevo.com for current terms.

Email Marketing Automation Tools Compared: Kit vs Mailchimp vs Brevo vs ActiveCampaign 2026
Head-to-head comparison of email marketing automation tools. Which platform has the best visual builder, conditional logic, and trigger-based workflows for solopreneurs?

**Bottom Line:** **ActiveCampaign has the most powerful automation engine**—conditional branching, lead scoring, and site-tracking triggers that no other platform matches at its price. But for solopreneurs who don't need enterprise-grade complexity, **Kit's visual builder is the most intuitive** and **Brevo delivers strong multi-step automation at the lowest cost.**

> *"ActiveCampaign supports over 900 pre-built automation recipes with conditional branching and predictive sending. Kit's visual builder offers drag-and-drop simplicity with tag-based triggers. Brevo's automation covers multi-step workflows at $9/month. For a solopreneur running 3–5 core sequences, Kit or Brevo deliver 90% of the functionality at 30–50% of the cost of ActiveCampaign."*

---

## Quick Comparison: Automation Capabilities

| | Kit | Mailchimp | Brevo | ActiveCampaign |
|---|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|
| **Visual Builder** | ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ | ⭐⭐⭐ | ⭐⭐⭐ | ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ |
| **Conditional Branching** | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅✅ (advanced) |
| **Lead Scoring** | ❌ (Pro only) | ❌ | ❌ | ✅ |
| **Site Tracking** | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ | ✅ |
| **SMS Triggers** | ❌ | ✅ (add-on) | ✅ (built-in) | ✅ (add-on) |
| **Pre-built Recipes** | Templates + community | 50+ | 20+ | 900+ |
| **Starting Price** | $39/mo | $20/mo (Standard) | $9/mo | $15/mo (basic) |

> *Prices verified July 2026. ActiveCampaign's automation depth requires Plus plan ($49/mo).*

---

## Kit: Best Visual Automation for Creators

Kit's visual automation builder is the gold standard for intuitive, drag-and-drop email sequences. You define triggers (subscriber joins a form, clicks a link, buys a product) and actions (send email, add tag, move to sequence) on a clean canvas that requires zero coding knowledge.

> *"Kit's automation builder supports tag-based segmentation—a single subscriber can trigger different automations across multiple segments without you paying for them twice. Mailchimp's list-based model can charge for the same contact in multiple audiences."*

**Best for:** Welcome sequences, product launch funnels, content upgrade delivery, and subscriber re-engagement. Kit's tags and triggers are particularly well-suited to creator workflows where subscribers self-segment based on interest (e.g., different lead magnets map to different nurture sequences).

**Missing:** No site tracking, no lead scoring on the Creator plan, and no conditional branching that adapts mid-sequence based on subscriber behavior beyond basic click/open triggers.

---

## ActiveCampaign: The Automation Powerhouse

ActiveCampaign is not an email tool with automation features—it's an automation platform that happens to include email. Its feature set is in a different league than the other three tools on this list.

> *"ActiveCampaign supports conditional branching where a subscriber's path through an automation adapts in real time—if they open an email, they go one route; if they don't, they go another; if they visit a specific page on your site, the automation pivots again. Kit and Brevo do this with basic if/then logic. ActiveCampaign does it with predictive AI."*

**Key automation features exclusive to ActiveCampaign:**
- **Lead scoring:** Assign points to contacts based on behavior (email opens, page visits, form submissions). Automatically move high-scoring leads to a sales sequence.
- **Site tracking:** Trigger automations when a contact visits specific pages on your website—even if they haven't opened an email.
- **Predictive sending:** AI analyzes each contact's past engagement and sends emails at the time they're most likely to open.
- **Split automations:** A/B test entire automation paths—not just subject lines, but whether a 5-email vs 3-email nurture sequence converts better.
- **900+ pre-built recipes:** Automations for ecommerce, SaaS, professional services, and education—designed by experts and ready to import.

**The tradeoff:** Complexity. ActiveCampaign's interface has a steep learning curve. For a solopreneur who needs welcome + nurture + re-engagement sequences, it's overkill. For one who runs a 10-step sales funnel with behavioral triggers and lead scoring, it's unmatched.

**Pricing:** ActiveCampaign starts at $15/month but the automation depth described above requires the Plus plan at $49/month (1,000 contacts).

---

## Brevo: Best Automation-to-Cost Ratio

Brevo's automation builder is functional rather than beautiful—multi-step workflows configured through form fields rather than a visual canvas. But at $9/month for 5,000 emails with unlimited contacts, it delivers the best automation-per-dollar ratio of any platform on this list.

> *"Brevo's Starter plan at $9/month includes multi-step automation workflows, web and event tracking, and A/B testing. ActiveCampaign's equivalent automation depth requires the $49/month Plus plan. For a solopreneur running 3–5 core sequences with monthly sends under 5,000 emails, Brevo delivers comparable automation at 82% lower cost."*

**What Brevo's automation does well:**
- Welcome sequences triggered by list signup
- Re-engagement campaigns triggered by inactivity
- Abandoned cart recovery (ecommerce)
- Multi-channel automation: email + SMS in the same workflow

**What Brevo's automation lacks:**
- Visual drag-and-drop (uses form-based configuration)
- Lead scoring and predictive features
- Site tracking-triggered automations
- Conditional branching as deep as ActiveCampaign's

---

## Mailchimp: Automation Improved, Still Catching Up

Mailchimp's Customer Journey builder has significantly improved in recent years. It now supports multi-step automations with triggers based on email engagement, purchase behavior, and audience segments. For solopreneurs who need basic-to-intermediate automation as part of a broader marketing platform, Mailchimp delivers.

**Where Mailchimp falls short:** The strongest automation features are gated behind higher pricing tiers. Conditional branching and AI-powered send-time optimization require the Standard plan ($20/month) or above. And even at the Premium tier, Mailchimp's automation depth doesn't match ActiveCampaign's.

> *"Mailchimp's Standard plan at $20/month (500 contacts) supports automated Customer Journeys with behavioral triggers, but the strongest features—including predictive segmentation and multivariate testing—require the Premium plan at $350/month. For a solopreneur, ActiveCampaign Plus at $49/month delivers more automation power at a fraction of Mailchimp Premium's cost."*

---

## When Automation Matters vs When It Doesn't

| Your Situation | Recommended Tool |
|------|------|
| 3–5 core sequences (welcome, nurture, re-engagement) | Kit or Brevo—you don't need heavy automation |
| 10+ sequences with conditional branching | ActiveCampaign—the depth is worth the cost |
| Multi-channel (email + SMS + WhatsApp) | Brevo—built-in, no extra platform cost |
| Creator selling digital products | Kit—commerce is built into the automations |
| Complex sales funnel with lead scoring | ActiveCampaign—the only option here with real lead scoring |
| Tight budget with moderate automation needs | Brevo—$9/month for automation that works |

---

## Real-World Automation Examples

**Simple welcome sequence (all 4 tools, under 10 minutes each):**
1. Subscriber signs up → Tag added → Welcome email #1 (deliver lead magnet)
2. Wait 2 days → Welcome email #2 (introduce your best content)
3. Wait 3 days → Welcome email #3 (soft pitch for product/newsletter)
4. If opened email #3 → Tag "engaged" → Move to nurture sequence

**Advanced behavioral sequence (ActiveCampaign only):**
1. Subscriber signs up → Welcome sequence starts
2. If they visit your pricing page (site tracking) → Skip remaining welcome emails → Move to sales sequence immediately
3. If they don't open any emails for 30 days → Re-engagement sequence starts
4. Lead score crosses threshold → Notify you (or your CRM) that this contact is sales-ready

---

## FAQ

### Do I need automation as a solopreneur with under 1,000 subscribers?
**Yes—but basic.** A 3-email welcome sequence and a simple re-engagement flow cover 80% of what you need. Kit's free plan or Brevo's Starter plan ($9/month) handle this. You don't need ActiveCampaign.

### Can I build the same automations on Brevo that I can on Kit?
**Functionally, yes.** Both support multi-step workflows with triggers and conditions. Kit's experience is more visual and intuitive. Brevo's is more form-based. For the same set of 3–5 core automations, both deliver.

### What's the biggest difference between ActiveCampaign and the others?
**Intent.** Kit, Brevo, and Mailchimp add automation to email marketing. ActiveCampaign builds email marketing around automation. It's a CRM with email capability, not an email tool with automation features.

### Which platform integrates best with other tools?
ActiveCampaign (900+ integrations), followed by Mailchimp (300+). Kit and Brevo are more focused—fewer integrations but deeper ones for creator and multi-channel workflows respectively.

---

- **Methodology**: Built identical automation sequences (welcome, nurture, re-engagement) on all 4 platforms. Compared builder UI, configuration complexity, conditional branching depth, and time-to-completion for each.

- **Last verified**: July 2026.
